title: Build a Device Inventory from CSV

problem.statement: You're given CSV text with columns hostname,ip,role,site. Fill in the loop body of csv_to_inventory so it returns a dict keyed by hostname (each value a dict of the remaining fields), skipping any row where the IP is not a valid IPv4 address.
problem.use_case: Turning a spreadsheet export into a clean inventory dict — with bad rows filtered out instead of silently breaking a downstream Netmiko/Ansible run — is one of the most common first automation scripts a network team writes.

hints: Call the provided _is_valid_ipv4(row['ip']) to decide whether to keep the row.
The hostname becomes the outer dict key, so pop it out of the row dict before storing the rest.

solution.explanation: Validate the row's IP with _is_valid_ipv4(row['ip']), and only for valid rows pop 'hostname' out (row.pop('hostname')) to use as the dict key, storing the rest of the row as its value: inventory[hostname] = row.
